steps.title: Calculate AC and identify the right triangle configuration
steps.content:
We're given $AB = 16$ and $AC = AB + 25 = 16 + 25 = 41$.

Triangle $ABC$ has a right angle at $B$, which means $AC$ is the hypotenuse. The altitude $BD$ is drawn from the right-angle vertex $B$ perpendicular to the hypotenuse $AC$ at point $D$.

steps.title: Apply the area method to find BC/BD
steps.content:
The area of a right triangle can be calculated two ways:
  • Using the two legs: $\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot AB \cdot BC$
  • Using the hypotenuse and its altitude: $\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot AC \cdot BD$
Since both expressions equal the same area:
$$AB \cdot BC = AC \cdot BD$$
Rearranging to isolate our desired ratio:
$$\frac{BC}{BD} = \frac{AC}{AB} = \frac{41}{16}$$
That confirms D is correct.
